name: simple-llm-models-taxonomy-2026-03
description: A practical taxonomy of widely used LLM-related models.

nodes.name: providers
nodes.description: Top-level grouping: all models are organized under their provider.

children.name: openai
children.description: OpenAI model catalog grouped by family and utility class (LLMs, embeddings, moderation).

children.name: llm-families
children.description: OpenAI LLM families used for chat, coding, reasoning, and multimodal tasks.

children.name: gpt-family
children.description: General-purpose GPT models used for chat, instruction following, tool use, and multimodal tasks.

children.name: gpt-4.1
children.description: Flagship GPT family variant commonly used for high-quality generation, coding, and complex instruction following.

children.name: gpt-4.1
children.description: Primary flagship variant.

children.name: gpt-4.1-mini
children.description: Cost-optimized smaller variant for high-throughput chat, extraction, and lightweight coding.

children.name: gpt-4.1-nano
children.description: Fastest and cheapest GPT-4.1 variant. 1M token context window. Optimised for classification, autocompletion, instruction following, and tool calling. Beats GPT-4o mini on key benchmarks. API-only (not available in ChatGPT).

children.name: gpt-4o
children.description: Omni/multimodal GPT model family commonly used for text + vision and interactive assistants.

children.name: gpt-4o
children.description: High-capability multimodal model for text and vision tasks.

children.name: gpt-4o-mini
children.description: Lower-cost multimodal model for fast assistants, summarization, and basic vision understanding.

children.name: gpt-4-turbo
children.description: Prior-generation high-capability GPT-4 variant often referenced in existing systems.

children.name: gpt-4-turbo
children.description: High-quality model used for chat and tool use; commonly treated as legacy/transition depending on availability.

children.name: gpt-3.5
children.description: Older, cost-effective text-only family widely used historically for chat and classification.

children.name: gpt-3.5-turbo
children.description: Popular legacy chat model for basic assistants and extraction; often replaced by newer mini variants.

children.name: o-series-family
children.description: Reasoning-optimized models used for complex multi-step problem solving, planning, and hard coding tasks.

children.name: o1
children.description: High-end reasoning model family for difficult tasks requiring deeper deliberation.

children.name: o1
children.description: Primary reasoning model for maximum quality on hard tasks.

children.name: o1-mini
children.description: Faster, cheaper reasoning model for moderate complexity tasks and higher throughput.

children.name: o3
children.description: Next-generation reasoning family positioned for strong reasoning and tool use.

children.name: o3
children.description: Primary o3 reasoning model.

children.name: o3-mini
children.description: Cost-optimized o3 variant for faster reasoning workloads.

children.name: gpt-5.x-family
children.description: Latest 5.x generation GPT family models, positioned above 4.x. Reasoning-class models that follow o-series API conventions.

children.name: gpt-5
children.description: Primary 5.x flagship variant.

children.name: gpt-5-mini
children.description: Cost-optimized 5.x variant for high-throughput assistants, extraction, and lightweight coding.

children.name: gpt-5-nano
children.description: Fastest, most cost-efficient GPT-5 variant. Optimised for classification, data extraction, ranking, and sub-agent workloads where speed and cost matter most. API-only.

children.name: gpt-5.1
children.description: Incremental 5.x revision. reasoning_effort defaults to none — must be set explicitly to enable reasoning.

children.name: gpt-5.1-mini
children.description: Cost-optimized 5.1 variant.

children.name: gpt-5.2
children.description: Incremental 5.x revision.

children.name: gpt-5.4
children.description: Incremental 5.x revision.

children.name: gpt-5.4-mini
children.description: Cost-optimised GPT-5.4 variant. Significantly faster than GPT-5.4 (2x+). Approaches GPT-5.4 on SWE-Bench Pro and OSWorld-Verified. Handles coding, tool use, multimodal, and sub-agent workloads. Available in ChatGPT Free and Go tiers.

children.name: gpt-5.4-nano
children.description: Fastest and cheapest GPT-5.4 variant. Designed for classification, data extraction, ranking, and sub-agent tasks where latency and cost are the primary constraints. API-only (not in ChatGPT consumer app).

children.name: anthropic
children.description: Anthropic Claude model families.

children.name: llm-families
children.description: Anthropic Claude families grouped by generation and tier.

children.name: claude-3-family
children.description: Claude 3 generation models. Legacy; superseded by 3.5 and later.

children.name: claude-3-opus
children.description: Highest-capability Claude 3 tier. Retired/deprecated.

children.name: claude-3-sonnet
children.description: Balanced Claude 3 tier. Legacy; superseded by claude-3.5-sonnet.

children.name: claude-3-haiku
children.description: Fast, lower-cost Claude 3 tier. Legacy; superseded by claude-3.5-haiku.

children.name: claude-3.5-family
children.description: Claude 3.5 generation models.

children.name: claude-3.5-sonnet
children.description: Strong coding and general assistant quality; widely used production model.

children.name: claude-3.5-haiku
children.description: Faster, cost-optimized tier for throughput-sensitive assistants and extraction.

children.name: claude-3.7-family
children.description: Claude 3.7 generation; introduced hybrid/extended thinking.

children.name: claude-3.7-sonnet
children.description: Hybrid-reasoning Sonnet model; supports extended thinking mode.

children.name: claude-4-family
children.description: Claude 4 generation (May 2025); professional-grade coding and agent capabilities.

children.name: claude-opus-4
children.description: Highest-capability Claude 4 tier.

children.name: claude-sonnet-4
children.description: Balanced Claude 4 tier.

children.name: claude-haiku-4
children.description: Fast, cost-efficient Claude 4 tier.

children.name: claude-4.5-family
children.description: Claude 4.5 generation (November 2025).

children.name: claude-opus-4.5
children.description: Top-tier Claude 4.5 model.

children.name: claude-sonnet-4.5
children.description: Production-default Sonnet; industry-leading coding and computer use.

children.name: claude-haiku-4.5
children.description: Fastest, most cost-efficient Claude 4.5 tier.

children.name: claude-4.6-family
children.description: Claude 4.6 generation (February 2026); current latest generation.

children.name: claude-opus-4.6
children.description: Most capable production model as of March 2026; adaptive thinking, 1M token context, 128k max output.

children.name: claude-sonnet-4.6
children.description: Latest Sonnet; near-Opus 4.5 coding quality at Sonnet pricing. 1M context window in beta.

children.name: google
children.description: Google DeepMind Gemini model families.

children.name: llm-families
children.description: Gemini LLM families grouped by generation.

children.name: gemini-2.0-family
children.description: Gemini 2.0 generation. Retiring June 1, 2026.

children.name: gemini-2.0-flash
children.description: Multimodal performance for general-purpose tasks. Retiring June 1, 2026.

children.name: gemini-2.0-flash-lite
children.description: Streamlined, ultra-efficient variant. Retiring June 1, 2026.

children.name: gemini-2.5-family
children.description: Gemini 2.5 generation. Current stable production generation.

children.name: gemini-2.5-pro
children.description: Flagship reasoning model. 1M token input context, 65536 output.

children.name: gemini-2.5-flash
children.description: Optimised for low-latency, high-throughput. 1M context. Default for most developers.

children.name: gemini-2.5-flash-lite
children.description: Built for massive scale. Most cost-efficient 2.5 tier.

children.name: gemini-3-family
children.description: Gemini 3 generation (late 2025–2026). Current frontier.

children.name: gemini-3.1-pro
children.description: Latest reasoning-first flagship. Adaptive thinking, 1M context, 64k output.

children.name: gemini-3-flash
children.description: Frontier-class performance at a fraction of Pro cost.

children.name: gemini-3.1-flash-lite
children.description: Most cost-efficient Gemini 3 tier. Free tier available.

children.name: xai
children.description: xAI Grok model families. OpenAI-compatible API (api.x.ai).

children.name: llm-families
children.description: Grok LLM families grouped by generation.

children.name: grok-3-family
children.description: Grok 3 generation (February 2025). Knowledge cutoff: November 2024.

children.name: grok-3
children.description: Grok 3 flagship. 131072 token context.

children.name: grok-3-mini
children.description: Cost-optimised Grok 3 variant. Supports reasoning_effort. 131072 context.

children.name: grok-4-family
children.description: Grok 4 generation (mid-2025). Current flagship. 2M token context. Knowledge cutoff: November 2024.

children.name: grok-4
children.description: Grok 4 primary flagship. Deep reasoning, 2M context.

children.name: grok-4-fast
children.description: Non-reasoning Grok 4 variant. 40% fewer thinking tokens. 2M context.

children.name: grok-4.1-fast
children.description: Latest Grok 4.1 Fast. Recommended default ($0.20/$0.50 per 1M tokens). 2M context. Reasoning and non-reasoning variants.

children.name: grok-code-fast-1
children.description: Specialised reasoning model for agentic coding. 256k context.

children.name: grok-4.20
children.description: Newest flagship (early access March 2026). logprobs field ignored.

children.name: deepseek
children.description: DeepSeek model families. Open-weight and API-hosted (api.deepseek.com). OpenAI-compatible API.

children.name: llm-families
children.description: DeepSeek families grouped by architecture generation.

children.name: v3-family
children.description: DeepSeek V3 generation. General-purpose MoE (671B params, 37B activated). Non-reasoning.

children.name: deepseek-v3
children.description: Original V3 release (December 2024). Open weights. 64K context.

children.name: deepseek-v3-0324
children.description: March 2025 post-training update. Improved reasoning and tool use via R1 RL techniques.

children.name: r1-family
children.description: DeepSeek R1 generation. Always-on CoT reasoning. Sampling params unsupported.

children.name: deepseek-r1
children.description: Original R1 (January 2025). Open weights. 64K context.

children.name: deepseek-r1-0528
children.description: May 2025 R1 update. Stronger reasoning. 64K context.

children.name: v3.2-family
children.description: DeepSeek V3.2 (December 2025). Current production. Hybrid thinking/non-thinking. 128K context.

children.name: deepseek-chat
children.description: Stable alias for V3.2 non-thinking mode. Recommended for chat, coding, and tool workflows.

children.name: deepseek-reasoner
children.description: Stable alias for V3.2 thinking mode. CoT in reasoning_content field. Omit temperature/sampling params.
